marketState: bearish-leaning, oversupply-driven but cushioned by hot near-term weather demand
summary: Storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and price is running -11.4pp below its seasonal median (-14.8% YTD vs -3.4% median), though the seasonality signal itself is flagged Low reliability (50pp spread) and only 31% odds of higher prices in 4 weeks. Daily and weekly technicals are uniformly bearish (50<200 EMA cross, negative MACD, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) and managed money is net short at the 35th percentile. The main offsetting factor is weather: 1-7D and 8-16D demand are running +15.4% and +8.4% above 5-year norms with high range position (0.82/0.69), which is keeping the front month roughly FAIR versus modeled fair value rather than outright cheap. The futures curve shows slight near-front backwardation (Aug>Sep) but steep contango into Dec/Jan, showing the market already pricing a normal winter premium.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.98 (scale in on bounces toward fair value)
longTerm.stop: $3.15 (above Nov-26 contract pivot; would break the bearish structure)
longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y) with a bearish surprise, seasonality below median with a negative skew (only 31% odds of higher prices in 4 weeks, typical -3.1% 4w move), and uniformly bearish daily/weekly technicals with a 50<200 EMA cross all argue for further downside; COT managed money is already net short but only at the 35th percentile, leaving room to add to shorts.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained daily close above $3.10-3.15, a swing to storage deficits vs 5y, or a material acceleration in weather-driven demand that erodes the storage surplus quickly.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially FAIR (spread -$0.015 vs $2.917 fair) so there is little near-term valuation edge, and timeframes disagree: 5m/15m are overbought (RSI 74.4/60.4, price above the upper 5m Bollinger) while 1h-1w remain bearish, and hot weather demand (+15.4%/+8.4% vs 5y) is providing support that offsets the bearish storage backdrop.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a rejection near $3.00-3.05 (short entry, aligning with the bearish daily/weekly trend) or a pullback to $2.70-2.75 support with weather still hot (countertrend long entry)

keyDrivers: Storage +184 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build
Seasonality -11.4pp below median, negative skew into 4 weeks, but reliability flagged Low
Weather demand running +15.4% (1-7D) / +8.4% (8-16D) above 5y norms, near top of range
Daily/weekly technicals bearish across the board (50<200 EMA cross, negative MACD)
COT managed money net short (-60,295) at 35th percentile vs 5y, room to extend
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (50pp spread) — the seasonal edge could reverse quickly
Weather beyond the 8-16 day window is uncertain; an extended heat wave or early cool snap could swing demand and price fast
Steep winter contango (Dec/Jan near $3.8-4.3) shows the market already pricing cold-winter risk; an early cold snap could spike the front month sharply against short positions
